summary: Documented and lightly type-tightened known game-mode-related struct fields across gm, mn, gr, lb, if, and player code. The PR converted several anonymous numeric or enum-like uses into clearer names or types, including slot_type, Gm_PKind_Human/Gm_PKind_Cpu, boolean language predicates, timer-frame comments, match-rule bitfield comments, and player/rules flags such as rumble enabled, invisible, friendly fire, single-button mode, and Zelda/Sheik transform-on-load.
what_changed: Added comments for known fields in gm structures, including lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t.x5 as match mode, x6 as is teams, lbl_8046B6A0_t.unk_B as end graphic/SFX type, unk_2C as timer frames, FighterMatchInfo.x4_b2 as invisible, and x4_b4 as Zelda/Sheik transforming on load.

Added comments for known menu/start-melee rule fields in src/melee/mn/types.h, including rumble enabled, invisible, CPU type, offense ratio, defense ratio, timer counts up, friendly fire on, single-button mode enabled, end graphic/SFX type, and multiple match callback function pointers.
Changed gm_8016B8D4's second parameter from s32 arg1 to u8 slot_type in both implementation and header, removed the local u8 truncation variable, and passed slot_type directly to grStadium_801D3B4C.
Removed explicit '& 0xFF' casts at gm_8016B8D4 call sites in ifstatus.c now that gm_8016B8D4 takes a u8 slot_type.
Renamed grStadium_801D3B4C's second parameter from arg1 to slot_type and documented grStadium_Display.xF0 as the slot type of the focused player.
Replaced raw slot type comparisons in mncharsel.c, changing checks like type == 0 || type == 1 to Gm_PKind_Human || Gm_PKind_Cpu.
Changed lb language predicate APIs from enum_t to bool in lblanguage.c and lblanguage.h, returning true/false for JP/US checks.
Changed a magic 0x3C frame threshold to decimal 60 in gm_16AE.c for timer-frame logic.
Changed x4_b4 assignment from 1 to true when marking Zelda/Sheik transforming on load.
Removed the unused struct gm_8016A92C_arg0_t from gm/types.h.
Removed an unnecessary void* cast from gmMainLib_8015EDA4() when assigning to u16* stage_unlock_mask.
Documented StaticPlayer bit b0 as rumble enabled.
smart_moves: Used domain enums such as Gm_PKind_Human and Gm_PKind_Cpu instead of raw 0/1 slot-type comparisons, making future matching and behavior review less dependent on magic numbers.
Tightened a parameter to u8 slot_type at the API boundary for gm_8016B8D4, eliminating repeated caller-side masking and a temporary truncation variable while preserving the intended byte-sized value.
Documented fields in-place on the structs that carry the data rather than only at use sites, making the discovered semantics reusable across unrelated systems.
Converted predicate-style language functions to bool, aligning return type with actual semantics and allowing callers/readers to distinguish boolean tests from general enum values.
Replaced boolean-like writes with true and frame-count magic with 60, improving semantic clarity without large rewrites.
Kept the cleanup small and cross-referenced: gm, ifstatus, grstadium, mncharsel, and type headers were updated together so slot type documentation and usage stayed consistent.
decomp_lessons: When a field's meaning is known but a full rename may be risky or broad, adding precise inline comments to the struct is still valuable decomp progress.
If a function only needs a slot type byte, encode that in the signature and remove ad hoc caller-side masks or local truncation variables.
Replace raw enum values with known enum constants as soon as the mapping is established; this documents behavior and reduces future accidental misuse.
Boolean predicates should use bool return types and true/false literals where matching allows it; this clarifies intent in library APIs.
Document bitfields next to their declarations, especially gameplay rule and player flags, because bit positions are easy to confuse across StartMeleeRules, PlayerInitData, and runtime match structs.
Callback fields in large setup/rules structs benefit from comments describing lifecycle timing, such as pause, unpause, match start, pre-frame, post-frame, and match end.
Small semantic cleanups can span several subsystems when a shared struct field is involved; update producer, consumer, and type definition together.
naming_conventions: Use slot_type for player slot-kind parameters and fields when the value corresponds to Gm_PKind_*.
Use enum constants such as Gm_PKind_Human and Gm_PKind_Cpu rather than literal 0 and 1 for player slot kinds.
Use bool-returning names with Is/Has-style predicates, as in lbLang_IsSettingJP, lbLang_IsSettingUS, lbLang_IsSavedLanguageJP, and lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS.
Prefer comments like 'timer frames', 'match mode', 'is teams', 'rumble enabled', 'invisible', and 'friendly fire on' to preserve discovered semantics until full field renames are feasible.
Use true/false for bitfields or flags once their boolean semantics are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed gm_8016B8D4's parameter type to u8 so the implicit truncation formerly expressed by a local temporary and caller masks is represented by the function signature.
Removed '& 0xFF' from Player_GetPlayerSlotType call sites after the callee signature carried the byte-sized slot_type requirement.
Removed an unused temporary in fn_8016CD98 that only copied FighterMatchInfo[i].x1 before the actual nonzero check/decrement logic.
Replaced 0x3C with 60 in timer logic, indicating the value is a one-second frame count rather than an opaque hex constant.
Removed an unnecessary cast from gmMainLib_8015EDA4() to u16* because the function return type already matched the target use.
The diff suggests semantic cleanup rather than a new matched function; no review evidence was provided about exact assembly constraints.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the body/excerpt was empty, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_8046B6A0_t fields documented here, especially unk_B, unk_2C, x4_b2, and x4_b4.
Search for remaining raw slot type comparisons against 0 or 1 that should use Gm_PKind_Human or Gm_PKind_Cpu.
Search for remaining Player_GetPlayerSlotType() & 0xFF patterns after gm_8016B8D4 signature cleanup.
Investigate whether StartMeleeRules callback fields x38 through x50 were later given named typedefs or renamed fields.
Check whether lbLang predicate return-type changes required or enabled cleanup in callers outside this diff slice.
Search for other uses of grStadium_Display.xF0 to confirm all focused-player slot type behavior is consistently documented.
